title: Change blueprint — specification
question: Does this specification say what we will build — and what we will not?
lede: Gate A reviews the shape of the change before any plan exists. Approving it locks scope and non-goals; the implementation plan (Gate B) is written only after this gate closes.
gateQuestion: Approve this specification as the contract for the tag-filter change?
scope: Filter the skills catalog by a single ?tag= query parameter
Pure filterByTag(skills, tag) importable by Node tests and the browser
Tag chips on each catalog row become filter links
No-JS renders the full, unfiltered catalog
Unknown tags render the empty state chosen in interface-directions
nonGoals: No multi-tag or free-text search of any kind
No network calls; the 3-skill catalog stays static
No URL state beyond the single ?tag= parameter
No persistence of the last-used filter
acceptanceCriteria: node --test passes with filterByTag covered for null, matching, and no-match tags
Opening ?tag=git shows exactly the git-tagged skills
Opening ?tag=unknown shows the accepted empty state, not a blank page
Disabling JavaScript shows the complete catalog with working chip links
Back/forward restores the previous filter state
No new runtime dependency enters package.json
assumptions: The catalog stays small enough (≤ 20 skills) that client-side filtering is instant
visibleSkills() remains the single source of catalog data
Tag names remain single lowercase words

options.label: Approve the specification
options.consequence: Scope and non-goals lock; change-blueprint proceeds to the implementation plan (Gate B).

options.label: Request changes
options.consequence: The spec reopens with your notes; nothing downstream starts until it is re-reviewed.

options.label: Reject the direction
options.consequence: The change is shelved; the interview notes and this draft are preserved for a future attempt.

recommendation.reason: Scope matches the interview frontier exactly; every criterion is mechanically checkable.

unresolved: empty-state microcopy finalization is tracked in interface-directions
openStatusLabel: Gate A open — no resolution proposed yet
